title: eurybia macrophylla
intro: Eurybia macrophylla, also known as large-leaved Eurybia, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the northeastern United States and southeastern Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing pain. The health benefits of Eurybia macrophylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate colds and flu, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Eurybia macrophylla can be prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
